text: fat is 0.2g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for a fruit, this really comes down to sugar control.
carbs is 13.8g. fibre is where the section starts to wobble. for fruit, the tradeoff is sweetness versus fibre, satiety, and overall payoff.
protein is 0.3g. protein quality is clearly one of the selling points. for a fruit, the cleanest wins are low sugar and decent fibre.
vitamin c at 5% DV, vitamin a at 1% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.
potassium at 2% DV, magnesium at 1% DV, iron at 1% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.

on the plus side. hydrating whole-fruit package because water and whole-food structure make it easier to use well than juice or sweets. easy to use as a whole food because simple, recognisable, and realistic for everyday eating. brings a clean fruit identity because it still contributes something valuable even when it is not elite.

now the downsides. protein contribution is tiny because that is normal for fruit, but it still limits nutritional breadth. sugar can climb fast because a lot of fruits look healthy but still lean sweet. satiety is not especially strong because fruit often needs help from the rest of the meal.
Apple is B tier. Best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.
